items.headline: Sinking TSMC ADR Premium Offers Trading Window, UBS Desk Says
items.summary: The narrowing gap between Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s Taiwanese shares and its US-listed stock is creating a new trading opportunity, according to a UBS Group AG client note.

items.headline: Euro-Zone Private Sector Unexpectedly Shrinks as Services Slump
items.summary: Business activity in the euro area unexpectedly shrank for the first time since late 2024 due to a steep drop in the services sector as the Iran war weighs on consumers.

items.headline: Norway $2.2 Trillion Fund Lost 1.9% in Quarter Led by Tech Slide
items.summary: Norway’s wealth fund lost 1.9% in the year’s first quarter, dragged down by its investments in US technology stocks.

items.headline: China Pledges to Stabilize Fertilizer Market as Planting Begins
items.summary: China’s agriculture ministry said it will secure sufficient fertilizer supplies and stabilize prices, as the Iran war disrupts global markets for the key crop nutrient.

items.headline: Stocks Fall as Oil Climbs With Iran Talks in Limbo: Markets Wrap
items.summary: A record-breaking rally in US stocks came to a halt as peace talks in the Middle East remained in limbo, sending Brent crude further above $100 a barrel.

items.headline: Google's Liz Reid on Who Will Own Search in a World of AI
items.summary: Alphabet's head of search on adapting to the rise of LLMs.

items.headline: Odd Lots: Google’s Liz Reid on Search in an AI World (Podcast)
items.summary: Not too long ago, search engines were the dominant form of querying the internet. But that’s changing since the rise of large language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Google’s Gemini. More and more people are getting their online info through AI, effectively bypassing the search bars of old and creating a tension for large tech companies that offer AI models, but also make money from web traffic and search-related advertising. In this episode, we speak with Elizabeth Reid, VP of search at Googl

items.headline: France Probes Weather Data Tampering After Surge in Polymarket Bets
items.summary: France’s forecasting office flagged suspected tampering with weather sensors at the country’s largest airport and referred the case to police, after detecting unusual readings alongside heavy betting on a popular prediction market.

items.headline: Pension VBL Moves €2 Billion Housing Portfolio to Fund
items.summary: German public-sector pension fund Versorgungsanstalt des Bundes und der Länder, or VBL, is transferring residential properties worth more than €2 billion ($2.3 billion) into a new fund structure.

items.headline: Apollo Nears €1.4 Billion Deal for Forvia’s Interior Unit
items.summary: Apollo Global Management Inc. is nearing a deal to acquire the auto interiors business of Forvia SE, people familiar with the matter said.

items.headline: German Business Activity Unexpectedly Shrinks as Services Plunge
items.summary: German private-sector activity unexpectedly contracted as the Iran war triggered the biggest plunge in the services sector in more than three years.

items.headline: WH Smith Suspends Dividend as It Warns of Iran War Impact
items.summary: WH Smith Plc suspended its dividend and warned that the conflict in the Middle East is affecting its business in a fresh blow to a company still recovering from an embarrassing accounting error.

items.headline: Nokia Earnings Beat Estimates as AI Pivot Shows Promise
items.summary: Nokia Oyj reported first-quarter adjusted profit that beat analyst forecasts, as the Finnish mobile network equipment maker’s push into artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure begins to pay off.

items.headline: Tesla Supplier Yangzhou Nanopore Is Said to File for Hong Kong IPO
items.summary: Chinese battery materials maker Yangzhou Nanopore Innovative Materials Technology Ltd. has filed confidentially for an initial public offering in Hong Kong that could raise at least $200 million, according people familiar with the matter.

items.headline: UBS AT1 Investors Get a Win as Swiss Plans Hold Off On Reform
items.summary: As UBS Group AG blasted the Swiss government’s plans on tougher banking rules, its riskiest bonds gained in value thanks to one sentence in the announcement.

items.headline: Roche Sales Decline on Swiss Franc’s Appreciation
items.summary: Roche Holding AG’s sales fell in the first quarter, hurt by the Swiss franc’s appreciation against the dollar.

items.headline: Nestlé Sales Rise as Coffee and Snacks Outweigh Recall Hit
items.summary: Nestlé SA showed resilient sales growth in the first quarter, helping the Swiss foodmaker contain the fallout from the largest product recall in its history.

items.headline: Sainsbury’s Says Middle East Conflict Risks Hitting Profit
items.summary: J Sainsbury Plc said profit could slip this year as Britain’s second-largest grocer faces higher costs and consumer uncertainty caused by the conflict in the Middle East.

items.headline: US & Iran in Hormuz Standoff, ASML's New Gear 'Too Expensive' for TSMC | Daybreak Europe 4/23/2026
items.summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, oil continues to move higher with the US and Iran locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz. The White House says it's still waiting for a new peace proposal from Iran, but has not set a deadline. 

The world's biggest chipmaker TSMC says ASML's latest machines are too expensive, dealing a blow to Europe's most valuable company. ASML's most cutting-edge chipmaking machines cost upwards of €350 million ($410 million). 

And, EU leaders meet in Cyprus to address the mounting pressures facing the bloc. 

Today's guest: Negah Angha, King's College London, Visiting Fellow. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Insight with Haslinda Amin 4/23/2026
items.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Hyundai Motor Misses Estimates as Global Headwinds Hit
items.summary: Hyundai Motor Co. first-quarter earnings missed estimates as South Korea’s largest automaker grappled with US tariffs and cooling demand in key markets, while warning the Iran war is disrupting supply chains.

items.headline: Ethiopia’s Nascent Stock Exchange Adds Bank as Fourth Listing
items.summary: Ethiopia’s biggest privately held bank became the latest company to list on the the nation’s nascent stock market, bringing the number of listings to four.

items.headline: Roknifard: US Blockade Won't Yield Significant Results
items.summary: The US and Iran remain locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides effectively choking off traffic during an extended ceasefire. President Donald Trump said the truce agreed to on April 7 would stay in place indefinitely while Tehran says it has no plans to take part in negotiations imminently. Julia Roknifard, Senior Lecturer at the School of Law & Governance at Taylor's University in Malaysia spoke to Bloomerg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the likely scenario. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Iran Concerns Give Oil a Short-lived Boost | The China Show 4/23/2026
items.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Azour: Prolonged War Impact Deeper Than Oil and Gas
items.summary: The IMF's Middle East and Central Asia Director says a prolonged war and continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz could deepen economic disruptions well beyond oil and gas.  Jihad Azour spoke to Joumanna Bercetche about how the shock is reverberating across the Gulf and broader global trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: India Contracts Fertilizer at Nearly Double Pre-War Price
items.summary: India, the world’s top importer of urea, has booked the nitrogen-based fertilizer at sharply higher prices than in a previous tender as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and pushes global benchmarks higher.

items.headline: Yuan to Surpass Yen in Currency Options Trading, LCH Says
items.summary: The yuan is on track to overtake the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in the foreign-exchange options market, according to London-based clearing house LCH.

items.headline: Oil Extends Gain as Peace Negotiations Between US and Iran Stall
items.summary: Oil rose for a fourth day as the US and Iran remained locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks.

items.headline: Stocks and Bonds Drop as US-Iran Standoff Sends Oil Higher
SET President: IPO Pipeline Faces a Difficult Situation
items.summary: Stock Exchange of Thailand President Asadej Kongsiri discussed the IPO pipeline, saying it is in a difficult situation. He also noted that the Thai exchange plans to introduce “mini-gold” futures. He spoke with Haslinda Amin on Bloomberg Insight. (Source: Bloomberg)
